title: alchemilla vulgaris subvar. latiloba
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. latiloba, commonly known as lady's mantle, is a perennial plant belonging to the Rosaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant's medicinal properties also provide relief from menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, while its antioxidants help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The herb can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support women's health and promote relaxation.
